raw_consumer_statement:
In XXXX, I was charged {$30.00} from Best Buy/CBNA bank. This was not a charge that I made but a fraudulent purchase that someone else made. They alerted me to it, but when they called using a voice activated phone call, they requested the last four of my social, which I did not supply because I thought it was a fraudulent call trying to gain my personal information. 
I spoke to an actual individual in XXXX and they said that this was indeed on my card. I then filed a dispute. I received a letter from them indicating that they've reversed the charges. 
I filed a dispute with all three credit bureaus in XXXX. The credit bureaus reached out to Best Buy/CBNA bank and were told that the charge was accurate. 
I spoke to a XXXX and a XXXX from Best Buy/CBNA on XXXX and they said that it was a fraudulent charge and they 'were n't sure ' why someone would say that this was a valid charge. Meanwhile, my credit is now the lowest it has been.
